Government of India
Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare 
Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare 
Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group 
(CWWG) meeting as on 22.06.2026 
10.21.40.32
30-JUN-2026 10:55 AM
[IMAGE page_01_img_1]

metadata.source_pdf: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 22.06.2026.pdf

source_pdf: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 22.06.2026.pdf

text:
Key Insights 
1. Kharif Season Sowing Status:  
 Kharif sowing data collection has started from 05th June, 2026 and the sowing is in preliminary 
stage. As on 19 June 2026, the area sown under Kharif crops is 119.90 lakh ha (including 57.31 
lakh ha in Sugarcane) as compared to 117.95 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.   
   
2. Weather Update:  
a) 
Temperature: Maximum temperature is likely to be above normal in Madhya Maharashtra, East 
India, Rajasthan and many parts of Northeast India. 
b) Rainfall Distribution: From June 01– 14 June 2026, the All-India cumulative rainfall is 8% lower 
than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India:           
(-) 40%; (ii) Northwest India: 13%; (iii) Central India: (-) 55%; and South Peninsula: (-) 4%. 
 The seasonal trough at mean sea level from Punjab to Bihar across Haryana and Uttar Pradesh 
persists. 
 Very heavy to extremely Heavy rainfall likely over northeast India till 25th and over Sub-
Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim till 22nd June. 2026. 
 Moderate to severe thunderstorm activity accompanied by squally winds, with moderate to 
severe Lightning activity is likely over East India, Chhattisgarh, and Rajasthan during the next 
two days. 
 Duststorm activity likely over West Rajasthan on 22nd June. 
 Heat wave to severe Heat wave conditions likely over Vidarbha, East Madhya Pradesh, and East 
Uttar Pradesh for next 4-5 days and Heat Wave conditions over Chhattisgarh for next 3 days and 
Telangana for next 2 days. 
 Overall, rainfall activity is likely to remain below normal over most parts of the country except 
some parts of south peninsular India and north-east India where it will be near normal during the 
week. 
 
 
 
3.  Inputs Situation 
 
a) Water Reservoir Status: As on 18 June 2026, the total live water storage in 166 important 
reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 50.457 billion cubic meter, which is 27.49% 
of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 86.62 percent of 
last year's storage and 113.02 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the 
corresponding period. The overall storage position is less than the corresponding period of last year 
in the country as a whole but is better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period. 
b) Groundwater Levels: In January, 2026, around 75.8% of the country's monitoring stations 
exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more 
than 20 m are observed in around 13.5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western 
states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh. 
10.21.40.32
30-JUN-2026 10:55 AM

metadata.source_pdf: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 22.06.2026.pdf

source_pdf: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 22.06.2026.pdf

text:
The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and 
Chandigarh during January, 2026 has been significantly influenced by the over extraction of 
groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.  
About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in 
January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.  
The Groundwater Drought Indices indicate that in January, 2026, groundwater conditions across 
India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in 
respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions — 
notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, and Nagaland. 
 
 
 
c) Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products. 
 
 
4. Pests & Diseases: Intensity of pests and diseases at some fields has been reported to be above 
Economic Threshold Level for Maize -vegetative stage (Downy mildew (Peronosclerospora sorghi)) in 
Karnataka. The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring is 
being carried out. The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold 
Level 
 
 
 
10.21.40.32
30-JUN-2026 10:55 AM

text:
Details of each Segment 
1. 
RAINFALL 
 
During the week (11 June – 17 June 2026), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period 
Average (LPA) by 48%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01st June to 17 June 2026) is lower than LPA 
by 38%.  
 
     
    
       
 
Rainfall Forecast: 
 Overall, weaker southwest monsoon conditions are likely to prevail over the country during the 
Week. 
 Overall, rainfall activity is likely to remain below normal over most parts of the country during the 
week. 
  
  
  
TEMPERATURE: MIN & MAX 
 
Week 1 (19 June - 25 June 2026):  
 Gradual fall in maximum temperatures by about 2°C likely over Northwest India till 22nd June 2026, 
gradual rise by 2- 3°C for subsequent 3 days and no significant change thereafter. 
 No significant change in maximum temperatures likely over Maharashtra till 21th June and gradual 
fall by 2-3°C thereafter. 
10.21.40.32
30-JUN-2026 10:55 AM

text:
 Heat wave conditions very likely in isolated pockets over Chhattisgarh on 22nd & 23rd June; East 
Madhya Pradesh, Vidarbha during 22nd-25th June, East Uttar Pradesh during 22nd-24th June; 
Telangana on 22nd June; with severe heat wave conditions in isolated pockets during 22nd-24th June. 
 
Week 2 (26 June – 02 July 2026):  
 Low probability of Heatwave conditions likely over parts of northwest India (Rajasthan, Punjab, 
Haryana & Uttar Pradesh), and central India (Vidarbha and adjoining regions of North Telangana) on 
some days of week.  
 Above normal (+1.6 to +3.0°C) maximum temperatures are likely over many parts of the plains of 
northwest India, central India, north peninsular India, and East India. Near-normal weekly maximum 
temperatures (-1.5 to +1.5°C) are likely across the rest of the country during the week. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Advance of Southwest Monsoon 2026: 
 
 The Northern Limit of Monsoon continues to pass through 18°N/60°E, 18°N/65°E, 18°N/70°E, 
Harnai, Solapur, Hyderabad, Bhadrachalam, Koraput, Phulbani, Ranchi, Jamui, Muzaffarpur and 
28.3°N/83°E.

23rd June, 2026.

 
Cotton: About 17.13 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 22.82 lakh ha during the 
corresponding period of last year. 
 
Jute: About 6.22 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 6.09 lakh ha during the 
corresponding period of last year.

3. 
RESERVOIR STORAGE  
 
As on 18 June, 2026, the current live storage in 166 reservoirs was 50.457 BCM (27.49%) of total live 
capacity of 183.565 BCM. 
 122 reservoirs having storage more than 80% of normal storage. 
 119 reservoirs having storage 51% to 80%. 
 25 reservoirs having storage upto 50%. 
 
      
Source: CWC

text:
4. REMOTE SENSING BASED SOIL MOISTURE OBSERVATION 
 
Soil Moisture Observation  
 
Soil moisture during 13 June – 19 June 2026 is similar to the average of the past 10 years in 
major parts of Rajasthan, Punjab, Uttarakhand Uttar Pradesh, Haryana, Himachal Pradesh, 
Tamil Nadu, Kerala and J & K. 
 
Soil moisture is better than the average of the past 10 years in parts of Ladakh and pockets of 
eastern Rajasthan. 
 
It was lower than average of past 10 years in pockets of Maharashtra, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, 
Northern Karnataka, Telangana, Jharkhand, Southern West Bengal, Gujarat, Eastern & southern 
parts of MP  and in the Northeastern parts of Country. 
 
 
 
. 
 
 
10.21.40.32
30-JUN-2026 10:55 AM
[IMAGE page_10_img_1]

text:
Analysis of CPI and WPI for the month of May, 2026 
 
1. Inflation Scenario for the Month of May, 2026 (based on WPI) 
The annual rate of inflation based on All India Wholesale Price Index (WPI) number is 9.68% 
(provisional) for the month of May, 2026 (over May, 2025). 
2. WPI FOOD INDEX (Weight 24.99%)  
The Food Index consists of ‘Food Articles’ from Primary Articles major group, and ‘Manufacture of 
Food Products’ from Manufactured Products major group. It observed a YoY inflation of 4.49 per cent 
in May 2026, compared to 3.11 per cent in April 2026. 
3. Inflation Scenario for the Month of May, 2026 (based on CPI) 
 
The Consumer Price Index (CPI: Base Year 2024) based inflation rate has increased to 3.93% 
(provisional) in May, 2026 from 3.48% in April, 2026. Consumer Food Price Index (CFPI) based 
inflation rate for All-India Combined has increased to 4.78% (provisional) in May, 2026 from 4.20% in 
April, 2026. 
4. A comparative study between the WPI and CPI inflation rates  
WPI (Base Year 2022-23) based inflation rate increased by 1.42% (from 8.26% in April, 2026 to 9.68% 
in May, 2026) and CPI based inflation rate increased by 0.45 (from 3.48% in April, 2026 to 3.93% in 
May, 2026).   
The WPI (Base Year 2022-23) inflation for May, 2026 at 9.68% which is higher by 9.88% from the 
same period last year (i.e. May, 2025 at -0.20%) and the CPI (Base Year 2024) inflation for May, 2026 
is at 3.93% while CPI (Base Year 2012) inflation for the same period last year in May, 2025 was at 
2.82%.  
On the other hand, WPI (Base Year 2022-23) based Food Index inflation rate for May, 2026 at 
4.49% which is higher than the same period last year (i.e. May, 2025 at 0.09%) and the Consumer Food 
Price Index (Base Year 2024) inflation rate for May, 2026 is at 4.78% while Consumer Food Price 
Index (Base Year 2012) inflation rate for the same period last year in May, 2025 was at 0.99%. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
10.21.40.32
30-JUN-2026 10:55 AM

source_pdf: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 22.06.2026.pdf

text:
Government of India 
Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare 
Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare 
Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group 
(CWWG) meeting as on 15.06.2026
[IMAGE page_01_img_1]

metadata.source_pdf: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 15.06.2026.pdf

source_pdf: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 15.06.2026.pdf

text:
Key Insights 
1. Kharif Season Sowing Status:  
 Kharif sowing data collection has started from 05th June, 2026 and the sowing is in preliminary 
stage. As on 12 June 2026, the area sown under Kharif crops is 84.60 lakh ha (including 54.08 lakh 
ha in Sugarcane) as compared to 88.04 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.   
 
2. Weather Update:  
a) 
Temperature: Maximum temperature is likely to be above normal in Madhya Maharashtra, East 
India, Rajasthan and many parts of Northeast India. 
b) Rainfall Distribution: From June 01– 14 June 2026, the All-India cumulative rainfall is 8% lower 
than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India:           
(-) 40%; (ii) Northwest India: 13%; (iii) Central India: (-) 55%; and South Peninsula: (-) 4%. 
 The seasonal trough at mean sea level from West Rajasthan to Gangetic West Bengal across 
northeast Rajasthan, north Madhya Pradesh, south Uttar Pradesh, Bihar and Jharkhand persists. 
 Isolated heavy to very heavy rainfall likely over Sub-Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim, 
Arunachal Pradesh and Assam & Meghalaya during next 6-7 days.  
 Rainfall activity is likely in South India, some parts of Maharashtra, NE India, some parts of 
Central India, J&K, and isolated places of Punjab, Haryana, Himachal Pradesh, and 
Uttarakhand. 
 Rainfall is likely to be above normal in Kerala, some parts of South Interior Karnataka, 
Arunachal Pradesh, while it is below normal in coastal Karnataka, Konkan-Goa, Gujarat, 
Odisha and East & Northeast India.  
 Isolated to Scattered rainfall likely over many parts of Northwest India and some parts of 
Central India during 14th - 20th June. 
 Heat wave conditions very likely in isolated pockets over Marathawada and Vidarbha during 
next 3-4 days. 
 
 
3.  Inputs Situation 
 
a) Water Reservoir Status: As on 11 June 2026, the total live water storage in 166 important 
reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 51.917 billion cubic meter, which is 28.28% 
of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 91.83 percent of 
last year's storage and 115.80 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the 
corresponding period. The overall storage position is less than the corresponding period of last year 
in the country as a whole but is better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period. 
b) Groundwater Levels: In January, 2026, around 75.8% of the country's monitoring stations 
exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more 
than 20 m are observed in around 13.5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western 
states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

metadata.source_pdf: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 15.06.2026.pdf

source_pdf: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 15.06.2026.pdf

text:
The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and 
Chandigarh during January, 2026 has been significantly influenced by the over extraction of 
groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.  
About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in 
January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.  
The Groundwater Drought Indices indicate that in January, 2026, groundwater conditions across 
India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in 
respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions — 
notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, and Nagaland. 
 
 
 
c) Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products. 
 
 
4. Pests & Diseases: The overall incidence of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold 
Level.
[IMAGE page_03_img_1]

source_pdf: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 15.06.2026.pdf

text:
Details of each Segment 
1. 
RAINFALL 
 
During the week (04 June – 10 June 2026), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period 
Average (LPA) by 26%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01st June to 10 June 2026) is lower than LPA 
by 26%. 
     
    
       
 
Rainfall Forecast: 
 Rainfall activity is likely in South India, West India, Vidarbha, some parts of Central India, 
Chhattisgarh, Odisha, NE India, J&K, some parts of Himachal Pradesh, isolated places in 
Uttarakhand. 
 Overall, rainfall is likely to be below normal over the entire country except Konkan & Goa, Madhya 
Maharashtra, Marathwada, Karnataka, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, Kerala, and Tamil Nadu, where it 
will be near normal to above normal during the week.  
  
  
TEMPERATURE: MIN & MAX 
 
Week 1 (12 June  - 18 June 2026):  
 Maximum temperatures is likely to be above normal over Rajasthan, East India, NE India, & West 
Coast. 
 Low probability of Heatwave conditions very likely in isolated pockets over Coastal Andhra Pradesh 
& Yanam during 11th-12th June; Telangana during 11th-13th June; Vidarbha during 11th-12th June.
[IMAGE page_05_img_1]
[IMAGE page_05_img_2]
[IMAGE page_05_img_3]

metadata.source_pdf: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 15.06.2026.pdf

source_pdf: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 15.06.2026.pdf

text:
 Hot and humid weather conditions likely to prevail over Konkan & Goa during 11th-13th June; 
Madhya Maharashtra during 11th-12th June. 
 
Week 2 (19 June - 25 June 2026):  
 Maximum temperatures is likely to be above normal NW India, East India, NE India, and Central & 
West India.  
 Low probability of Heatwave conditions likely over parts of northwest India (Rajasthan, Punjab, 
Haryana & Uttar Pradesh) on some days of the week. 
 No Warm Night conditions likely during the Week. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Advance of Southwest Monsoon 2026: 
 
 The Northern Limit of Monsoon continues to pass through 18°N/60°E, 18°N/65°E, 18°N/70°E, 
Harnai, Solapur, Hyderabad, Bhadradri Kothagudem, Kalingapatnam, Paradeep, Baripada, Purulia, 
Dhanbad, Muzaffarpur and 28.3°N/83°E as on 15th June, 2026.
[IMAGE page_06_img_1]
[IMAGE page_06_img_2]

metadata.source_pdf: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 15.06.2026.pdf

source_pdf: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 15.06.2026.pdf

text:
 Conditions are favourable for further advance of southwest monsoon into more parts of 
Maharashtra, remaining parts of Karnataka, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, West Bengal, some more 
parts of Odisha, Jharkhand, Bihar and some parts of South Chhattisgarh during next 4-5 days.
[IMAGE page_07_img_1]

  CROPS

 
Sugarcane: About 54.08 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 54.29 lakh ha during the 
corresponding period of last year.  
 
Cotton: About 9.53 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 13.19 lakh ha during the 
corresponding period of last year. 
 
Jute: About 6.18 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 6.09 lakh ha during the 
corresponding period of last year.

3. 
RESERVOIR STORAGE  
 
As on 11 June, 2026, the current live storage in 166 reservoirs was 51.917 BCM (28.28%) of total live 
capacity of 183.565 BCM. 
 126 reservoirs having storage more than 80% of normal storage. 
 15 reservoirs having storage 51% to 80%. 
 25 reservoirs having storage upto 50%.

text:
4. REMOTE SENSING BASED SOIL MOISTURE OBSERVATION 
 
Soil Moisture Observation  
 
Soil moisture during 06 June – 12 June 2026 is similar to the average of the past 10 years in 
major parts of Rajasthan, Gujarat, Madhya Pradesh, Maharashtra, Northern and Central 
Karnataka, Punjab, Bihar, Jharkhand, Uttarakhand, Uttar Pradesh H.P., Tamil Nadu, Kerala, 
West Bengal and J & K. 
 
Soil moisture is better than the average of the past 10 years in parts of Ladakh, pockets of 
Rajasthan and Northern West Bengal. 
 
It was lower than average of past 10 years in pockets of Odisha, Chhattisgarh, pockets of 
Haryana, Southern Karnataka, Maharashtra, Andhra Pradesh and in the Northeastern parts of 
Country. 
 
 
 
 
.
[IMAGE page_10_img_1]
